reviews.review_text:
I love this game so much, but sadly I will now stop playing for the time being after almost 3.8k hours and spending easily over 500–700 euros.
Six‑star lobbies in Europe are a complete ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥… hackers, ping abusers, and so on. In almost every round, enemy players seem to smell you from five miles away.
This game has so much potential and so much to offer, but getting demolished every round by extremely suspicious players is no longer bearable.

Don’t get me wrong: I’m completely fine with losing a fair gunfight, getting shot from behind, or being third‑partied. But dying to players who see you for 0.1 seconds and land the most questionable shots is just not okay.
I’ve been a six‑star player since before the 1896 update, and I know six‑star lobbies are sweaty — but not like this, not like in the last couple of months.

You can report players in‑game and on Steam, but it doesn’t matter. They can just keep playing without facing any consequences.
Sometimes they are even showing their cheats on their Steam Page for everyone to see.
And no, it shouldn’t be that the only way Crytek looks into hackers is when you report them through their website. That is pathetic in 2026, in my opinion, if that’s the only real way to get these players banned.

What would help the game?

Ping limit / region lock

Active devs who actually investigate suspicious players and ban them

It could be so simple, but it seems like Crytek doesn’t really care.

As far as I know, these problems are much more common in six‑star lobbies and less so in lower‑star brackets.
So if you’re new to the game or still in lower‑star lobbies: enjoy it, and hope you never reach six stars — they will ruin your Hunt days.

I can't come to a term that I will leave negative review for this game. It pains me so much that this game got twisted into something that was not meant to be. Game started as hardcore, gothic horror, extraction shooter set in 1895. When I say this I mean a REAL hardcore game. It used to be very hard and very bone chilling to play it. 

Old gold era:

There was no hand holding, boss arena was scariest place on map not because of boss, but because of players. Back then you did not know if someone was waiting for you to kill you and your teammates. You had to play slower thus why game match used to be 1 hour long. Nights used to be pitch black and frequent, it was a living nightmare to play night maps but that was a point! You always had this coldness in your body when you were walking on open or in swamps during the night. You would never know what lurks behind next tree or shack. Even regular grunts used to be scary enemy that could end you if you are not focused. From the start of match till the very end when you reach the exit, you were always under pressure and fear even if you killed lots of enemy hunters. There was just no way of knowing if you are 100% alone on the map. Not one time my heart rate dropped under 100 bpm and adrenaline rush was constant. THIS is why I bought this game so long ago, THAT is why back then all that I could do is scream "SHUT UP CRYTEK AND TAKE MY MONEY" after seeing this game and how it plays.

Bounty was a must go in every match every time. Why? Because game used to be hard so getting that sweet booty of bounty was important and if fact it was the only way to score big cash from the match (unless you got lucky and looted 1000$ from dead enemy but back then that was super rare). If you lose you felt it on your stash, if bounty escaped you still felt it on your stash, hunters were way, WAY more valuable and backing of the fight and leaving was not ashamed. In fact, many times, in old era of HS, players would say " Nah I want to keep this hunter and gear! We have no ammo, no equipment to fight and survive! We need to dip!". So you leave the map and actually LIVE TO FIGHT ANOTHER DAY! 

This is how Hunt should have stayed. A hardcore game in which you would be afraid, ultra focused, careful and pouring sweat from just being in game and not a CSGO type sweater where you mindlessly run around and gun down people.

Middle era:

Game started to be easier and faster. Boss whisper and red HUD mechanic, night maps became brighter and rarer, grunt started to be more of nuisance then actual threat but still they were dangerous. Traits were redesigned, some for good some for worse and new ones were added that made game more faster, more casual and less what game ground was. Match was cut to 45 minutes because of fast pace that we were getting in, 

Developers started to hand hold many players because they cried like babies and instead of actually learning how to deal with Hardcore game, they wanted papa Crytek to "fix" their lack of skills and ability to learn, by making more and more things easier for them. 

Still game was in somewhat perfect balance between old hardcore and what it is now so it was still ok in my book. Yes, even though I hated this changes that made game easier, I still loved the game with all my heart.

New era (post 1896):

This is when everything went down hill for this game. It lost its own soul and is no longer game that was supposed to be. Game became ultra fast, bounty became last thing that people would go to, everything is about shooting other players. 

First thing that you hear when match starts is this "Let's rush middle and see where other players are" , "Let's see if they are close so we can go fu*k them up" , " Who cares about bounty? I want to go and shoot people!". Hunt turned it self into Counter Strike. 

Economy became broken because before you had to go and get bounty for the money. Now with events, traits and all other mechanics you can get 3 times more money just buy killing players, looting cash registers and cash sacks. Losing hunter was not big deal anymore, because you can just get another one and go to event points to farm XP and speedrun them to level 50 with best traits you can get with no effort at all.

Events became life support for game but unfortunately that is not good. When there are no events, game becomes deserted with big portion of players leaving the game. And why wouldn't they? Playing outside of events is not profitable and thus game becomes little bit harder. But that is too much for them and so they leave and come back when there is another event.

Traits and cards became legal cheats in game that doesn't just hold hands of players but carries them. Cards that grant you wall hacks, lets you see through eyes of enemy player (this also gives you ability to see if you are last person on map), pinpoint direction of enemy players, fast heal, fast grunt killing (grunts became boring chore), fast everything. Almost everything that you do now leaves trace behind you for others to track you down easily. Exit points can now be blocked with these cards to make easier for pursuers (when pursuing actually happens), clown looking hunters that have nothing to do with Hunt world (Ghostface for example), UI has been reworked for worse in favor of consoles and it keeps getting worse.

Final words:

Game went from something hard, slow, actual skill required and unique masterpiece to another boring casual, ultra fast, sweater, shooter game like COD, CSGO and Battlefield, where you don't have to use more than 1 braincell to paly it. It is like if DayZ started to turn into Fortnite. This is not Hunt Showdown that I love and with that I, with my 1800+hours, retire from this game, maybe forever. I can only hope, but it will never go back to what it used to be. 

For all players who came here post 1896, all I can say is hope you enjoy this now casual arcade of the game for you would never last even a minute in old era, you tourists. But that's ok. We all have our own taste when it comes to games. Some players like to play games in which all you do is sprint, jump and shoot other players around the maps, using every game mechanic that makes it super easy, without requiring any skills or brain whatsoever. So if you are that kind of player, you will love this game.

But for us who don't like game to hold our hands and just love the thrill of using our brain while playing tactically, building skills, outsmarting the enemy, learning how to deal with game hardness of its own nature and enjoying the adrenaline rush that this game used to provide, then this version of game is no longer for us. We can only move on and let new kids play with their new baby sucker we call "Hunt Showdown 1896".

Goodbye Hunt Showdown, thank you for what you were long time ago but now you are just shadow of your former self. You are no longer worthy to be on my list of best game ever. See you in HUNGER!

Zbogom i dobar pogled! (Goodbye and have great hunt!)

As someone who plays in the Asia Server. I 100% do not recommend this game. You're sharing the server with the entire country of China which is riddled with cheaters and it's 100% acceptable and part of Chinese culture to cheat because it's part of life and living life means "to get ahead in life, no matter the cost". You should check out the revivews in Simplified Chinese as well. Constant negative reviews because there are actual Chinese gamers that actually wanna play games but they get cheaters constantly.
Suspicious gameplay, trash servers, rehashed content for nearly a year. Unfortunate. But hey, got nearly 2k hours out of it.

I had over 4000h in Hunt on console and I stopped playing for a year now because I really was sad about the state of the game and the devs messed everything up. Now i came back after a year, this time on PC and I am even more disappointed. The state of the game is horrible, the matchmaking (like everybody knows) is a joke. Menu? Yes, still a disgrace to any human being.
I played Hunt since it was released and it breaks my heart to see this game failing so HARD! I will now, after thousands and thousands of hours in this game, say goodbye for ever. Looking at what the devs did to this once masterpiece is a joke and i dearly hope that when HUNGER and Beautiful light release, this game will lose its base to better games and finally disappear.
Masterpiece ruined.

English:

Huge congrats to the Crytek team; over the last 8 years, you’ve somehow managed to consistently make the game worse under the guise of "updates." You probably thought you were pulling off a revolution by updating CryEngine and sending the CPU load through the roof, but all you did was kick out thousands of long-time players with older systems—or forced the rest of us into this optimization-deprived, disastrous experience. I guess you don't really care about ruining the game as long as you can squeeze more money out of the console market, but hey, did you at least pull it off?

It’s been all these years and you still haven't implemented a detailed analysis system that shows exactly who killed us how, from how many meters, with what ammo, or how we took/dealt damage. We’re literally out here Googling "Hunt map," squinting at screenshots, and trying to calculate coordinates ourselves just to figure out how many meters was the shot that we dealt. It’s unbelievable. At the end of a match, there's zero info, zero detail. We can’t even see a proper breakdown of the damage we dealt. "Your teammate downed this hunter" — Oh, really? Which teammate? How? Even putting that aside, at least let us see the stats for the players we downed: the distance, the weapon, the damage... If you kill someone and then die a bit later, that kill or damage doesn't even show up in the "Damage History." Instead of putting cowboy hats on flying bugs, how about you fix the thousands of "bugs" in this game? If you can't do it yourselves, get some help from players like RachtaZ who have put thousands of hours into this. You’ve even taken away our freedom to just load into a map solo to quietly explore and learn the layout. You break working systems and the UI with every single patch, then spend months "fixing" them and bringing them back in a broken state as if you’re doing us a huge favor. You call this innovation? The "Revive Bolt" move was the peak of this nonsense; it’s a miracle that I was quit at that time. And that "All Ears" trait? Being able to hear a player fart from 200 meters away was "brilliant." Oh wait, you removed it a week later because of the backlash. Maybe if you actually watched the people playing on the test servers... but where's the effort? Oh, and by the way, those "tracking" features you finally added after 8 years are great. Congrats, how did you manage that as a tiny indie studio? (!).

Even the core mechanics like hit feel and hit registration are dying. I hit a guy right in the chest with a Crossbow Shotbolt, I get the hit marker, but he takes zero damage. Even RachtaZ, one of your top players, says the Shotbolt is broken every time someone asks him on stream. I seriously wonder if anyone on your team actually plays this game once in a while. I land a full shotgun blast on someone from 10 meters (and no, not Pennyshot), and they take 60 damage. Shots just don't register. You must be renting your servers for free or something. You guys are constantly breaking this game with every patch. I won’t complain about bullet drop tho; I actually like that feature. Also no need to mention about cheaters, you guys can read thousands of reviews if you bother.  Between the CPU-hogging bad code and the features being stripped away every day, you're treating the players like idiots. Nobody asked for mind-blowing 4K RTX graphics; that’s not why people play this game. They play it because it’s unique and used to offer a truly great experience. Now, people just finish the Battle Pass and leave. Not that I think you care. Selling $5 Battle Passes and flashy weapon skin DLCs while keeping the game broken for 90 days only to "try" and fix it later shows exactly what kind of "high-quality" company you are. It’s a real shame to waste 8 years of progress with such incompetent management.

Türkçe:

Crytek ekibini tebrik etmek lazım; 8 yıldır bir oyunu, güncelleme adı altında ancak bu kadar istikrarlı bir şekilde geriye götürebilirdiniz. CryEngine’i güncelleyip işlemciye binen yükü arşa çıkarınca devrim yaptığınızı sandınız herhalde ama yaptığınız tek şey, yıllardır bu oyuna emek veren eski sistem sahibi binlerce insanı oyun dışı bırakmak ya da bizlere optimizasyon özürlü, rezalet bir deneyim sunmak oldu. Konsolda daha fazla para kazanmak için oyunun içine sıçmak pek umrunuzda değil sanırım, başarabildiniz mi bari?

Üstelik onca sene geçmesine rağmen hala kime, tam olarak  kaç metreden ve hangi mermiyle öldüğümüzü, nasıl veya neyden hasar aldığımızı veya birini nasıl vurduğumuzu gösteren detaylı bir analiz sistemini getiremediniz. Google a "Hunt map" yazıp resmen koklayarak vurduğumuz yerden vurduğumuz kişinin bulunduğu koordinatı hesaplayıp kendimizce analiz yapmaya çalışıyoruz, inanılmazsınız. Oyun sonunda bilgi sıfır, detay sıfır; kime ne kadar hasar verdiğimizi bile doğru dürüst göremiyoruz. "Takım arkadaşın bu avcıyı düşürdü" << Yok canım? hangisi düşürdü peki? nasıl düşürdü? hadi onu geçtim, bari bizim düşürdüğümüz/öldürdüğümüz oyuncuya ne ile hasar verdiğimizi, kaç metreden verdiğimizi, hangi silahla verdiğimizi öğrenelim. Oyun içinde birini öldürüp, bir süre sonra ölünce bile "Hasar Geçmişi" ekranındaki hasar kaydında öldürdüğümüz veya hasar verdiğimizin bilgisi yok. Uçan böceklere kovboy şapkası getireceğinize oyunda var olan binlerce sorunla ilgilenin. Beceremiyorsanız, RachtaZ gibi veya oyuna onun gibi binlerce saatini vermiş oyunculardan destek alın. Kendi başımıza bir haritaya girip, sükunetle etrafı keşfederek haritayı öğrenme özgürlüğümüzü bile elimizden aldınız. Update diye getirdiğiniz her yamada oyunun çalışan sistemlerini ve arayüzünü bozup, sonra o bozduğunuz her şeyi aylara bölerek teker teker, sanki büyük bir lütufmuş gibi bozuk haliyle geri getirmeyi anlaşılan sizler yenilik sanıyorsunuz. "Revive bolt" hamleniz zaten zirveydi, o dönem bırakmış olmam mucizeymiş. "All Ears" diye trait getirip, 200 metreden osuran bir oyuncuyu bile tespit edebilmek harikaydı, ha pardon, oyuncular tepki gösterince 1 hafta sonra kaldırdınız, test sunucusunda deneyimleyen oyuncuları izleseydiniz keşke, ama nerde. He bu arada 8 yıl sonra "tracking" feature adı altında getirdiğiniz şeyler güzeldi, tebrik ederim, nasıl başardınız minicik bir indie firma olarak? (!). 

Oyunun en temel mekaniği olan vuruş hissi ve kayıt sistemi bile artık can çekişiyor. Crossbow shotbolt ile adamın göğsüne vuruyorum, hit marker alıyorum ama düşman hasar almıyor. Oyunun en bilinen ve en iyi oyuncularından RachtaZ bile her yayınında soru sorulduğunda söylüyor "Shotbolt" bozuk diye, merak ediyorum sizin ekipten birileri oyunu hiç arada sırada falan oynuyorlar mı? Shotgun ile tüm vücuda saçma atıyorum(hayır pennyshot değil), 10 metreden, adam 60 hasar alıyor, vuruşlar kayıt edilmiyor. Serverları herhalde bedavaya kiralıyorsunuz. Her güncellemede oyunu bir şekilde bozmayı başarıyorsunuz. Bullet drop' a laf etmeyeceğim, sevdiğim bir özellik. Hile kullananlardan bahsetmeye gerek bile duymuyorum, o konuda binlerce inceleme var zaten, tabi üşenip okumak isterseniz. İşlemciyi sömüren kötü kodlama ve her geçen gün elimizden alınan özelliklerle oyuncuyu aptal yerine koyuyorsunuz. Hiç kimse sizden muhteşem grafikler sunmanızı, 4K RTX li oyun deneyimi istemedi, kimse zaten bu oyunu o yüzden  oynamıyor, bu oyunun eşi benzeri olmadığı için, gerçekten harika bir deneyim yaşattığı için oynuyor, oynuyor-DU. Şimdilerde sadece battle pass yapıp gidiyorlar. Gerçi sizin umrunuzda olduğunu da sanmıyorum, 5 dolarlık battle pass, cafcaflı silah skinlerine sahip DLC satarak, 90 gün oyunu bozarak ve sonrasında düzeltmeye çalışarak çok kaliteli bir firma olduğunuzu gösteriyorsunuz zaten. 8 yıllık birikimi bu kadar beceriksiz bir yönetimle harcamanız gerçekten yazık.

After 2500 hours in game I have finally uninstalled this. Its fun don't get me wrong but the devs don't do anything about the Ping Abusers, cheaters, or fix the ranking system. it makes the game unplayable when 4 out of 5 matches you are killed by someone cheating or the highest ranked player going into a half rank lobby. They are coming out with new content via events and battle passes but it just not worth the hassle of loading in game have your trio wiped via headshot through the wall at 200+m and sent back to lobby. It has only gotten worse over the years I have played and there are better games out there to enjoy your time on.
Completely ruined the game with the rampant monetization and destruction of the tone.
I've got so many hours in this game. Great game as an alternative to the run and gunners shooters that flood the market. All the weapons are viable, great gameplay loop.

Really interesting and well-supported game. 

Sadly, the community is a bunch of crustaceans and play like said creatures, making the game a boring test of "Who can be the biggest, fattest and most disgusting barnacle-ridden crab around". 

What a shame.
Class, tough but rewarding! harder than most fps but cool aesthetic and solid gameplay!

Great game, gets your blood flowing, and keeps you coming back for more.

Gunfights still feel tense even after 250 hours. The setting of the game allows for trading shots instead of getting full auto'ed by campers like in many other fps games. The extraction shooter aspect makes for rewarding wins and sometimes devestating loss streaks. Using the free hunters theres always a backup if you do run out of funds so youll never have to go in scaving like you'd have to in tarkov.

With regular updates still coming out and a massive meta overhaul incoming Id reccomend picking it up.

Developers consistently make nerfs and remove content to make it more "balanced" even if it makes it less fun. Their idea to increase players using items they dont like is to nerf "meta" and make gameplay harder to force you to use more items per round. Tired of devs thinking players enjoy meta nerfs

One of the best online PvPvE games in history

A quick piece of advice for newcomers: If you are coming from very fast-paced games, don't judge Hunt too quickly. Give it more time and patience. Once you understand the unique pacing and its incredible audio design, you will realize what a masterpiece this game is.

I keep giving this game a chance over and over. Hit registration is still horrible since I started 6 years ago. I can shoot someone within 20M twice (with rifle or pistol) and they are still alive.  Shotguns are hit or miss, I have snuck up on people 1M with their back turned to me and they still live. 

There are cheaters and they do not do anything about it.  Granted I do not run into hackers often but enough times to ruin the experience. If one person is hacking on the server you stand no chance. As many reports as people get on steam and in game, nothing happens. I will say that there are less obvious cheaters than before but they are still around.

This has potential to be one of the best games but the devs dont listen and keep running events and selling skins.

This is the final uninstall.
It's good but the automatic weapons game are a complete turn off for me I was expecting less COD and more cowboy fights.
They keep adding frustation elements and frankly ridiculous bs mechanics. I do not enjoy the game as I used to and it's getting worse with every update.

In the pool of extraction/battle royal game, Hunt is different, the louisiana from 1896 is a perfect backdropped, the gun play is more tense, every bullet counts. 

The big strenght of the game, it's sound design, it is just one of the best out there.

Is the game perfect ? no, but definitely a refreshing change from the other shooters all set in modern/futuristic conflict.

A lot can be said good and bad for the game, the community, and Crytek.

However, this game will not respect your time. Matches last about 45 minutes, maybe 3-4 minutes of which would have any meaningful gameplay. That is assuming you aren't killed at spawn or actually get to do something besides press W for 20 minutes then die. 

I just don't have it in me to sit and do nothing for 30-45 minutes on the slot machine roll of 3 minutes of good gameplay.

Long time ago... I saw this game it was 2018. I played beta version. it was so different. But this game has too many player. I can't belive that. its like PAYDAY 2 . Updates, skins, cosmics etc. But that's enough for me. I can't spend too much at game but I saw someone has a 9,000 hours. Woah! 

I think this game is not Legend for me but it could be legend like CS or something.

Crytek had a economic crysis but The Hunt saved them. 

Hey Cowboy or Hunter, What ever you want to be this game for u. Dark Fantasy waits u.

I have played this game since launch. It is easily my favourite shooter of all time. I did stop playing after the 2.0 update that many people say ruined the game, but I am happy to say that as of writing this review the game is in a very good state in my opinion. 

Graphically this game is beautiful, the environments and lighting make for a pretty game to look at. As for sound it is top notch every gun has a unique sound that make it easy after some time to tell what gun is being used by other players from the distance. Animations are all incredible and make for very satisfying gameplay.

The gameplay is where this game really shines though. You start by hiring a hunter and equipping them with a plethora of guns, tools and different consumables to fit your playstyle (Don't get attached to them they die permanently, though it isn't as unforgiving as other extraction shooters) . All of the guns are deadly, and have the potential to one shot enemy hunters with a well placed head shot making even the inexpensive non meta guns viable. You start a match on the edge of the map with up to 12 players yourself included. They could be a mix of solos, duos and trios depending on what you picked during matchmaking (Be warned there is no solo queue, if you play solo you have to at least contend with teams of two. Unless you play the soul survivor gamemode). When the match starts there will be one or two bounty targets that you must find by searching for clues or just randomly stumbling upon them. You can approach this in a number of ways. You can run around making noise not caring about being silent, or you could be stealthy and avoid sound traps that give away your position (crows, horses, dogs, branches, etc). Both are fun and you will probably end up doing a mix of both in any given game. You also get to decide if you want to be the one to kill and banish the bounty target, which lets everyone on the map know where it is, or would you rather wait for someone else to do the hard work and kill them afterwards. You "win" by extracting with the bounty, though you can extract anytime with or without the bounty. When you are carrying the bounty everyone can see you on the map so be prepared to fight. To counter this you have 5 seconds of dark sight, which is basically just wall hacks that lets you know where nearby hunters are. This leads to intense gun fights and incredible highs and devastating lows. 

In short I highly recommend this game if you are new or returning. If you are new I would watch some gameplay first to see if this game is right for you

Simply put, this game was fun and actually somewhat even skill wise. You just had to outthink the others but now its like everyone hits their shots on me but mine don`t register half the time and are not as effective even from the same exact loadout, distance, wind, rain, drag, fart smells, and even holding my mouth correctly. The constant spawn, die within 10-15 minutes from the first gun fight and repeat is just old. Some people aren`t full time gamers anymore and just want good even comp lobbies not whatever has been going on as of late. Make a dedicated "Dad Server" or something. After a 70hr work week man I just wanna relax with a decent game, not get one-shotted every match.

devs dont care about the core game. cheaters everywhere, particularly chinese and asians in general. done with this trash of a game. all they do is release skins (which look pathetic in game anyway due to poor texture rendering despite highest settings possible. stay away if you want to enjoy this game.
THIS GAME SUCKS SO MUCH, do not play if its not a new update and within the first few weeks of it. EVERYONE WHO PLAYS IS apparently a pro or cheating their a** off.

Love this game with all my heart, made many friends and we still run this game together often. youll need to stick to a weapon for a bit to understand its needs. bullet time and drop was a necessary addition to this game other wise it was just one shot regardless.

DO NOT BUY this game devs are not intressed to deal cheaters and ping abuse,servers are programmed that high ping matters ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ really!! easy anti cheat is joke like any other games google it,eu server are full of ping abuse and cheaters and eu server players has switched to us east for fair atleast somehow fairplay
It is fun game, I love being a cowgirl and having fun in lobbies 🤠

they slowly removed everything good about the game since launch (music and ui/menu not excluded)

also sbmm was a choice that was made so you can face the same 5 people on repeat

One word can describe my Hunt experience: Inconsistent.

One in five or so games will be stable and I can make it through till completion or death of my hunter, but most of my time in this game has been spent fighting connection issues and dealing with ping abuse.
 
This game's servers are easily the most unstable I've seen in a long time, which is a shame considering the game's been out for 8 years. It seems like no matter how good my internet connection is, I cannot get a stable game on the US West servers to save my life. 

Often I have to attempt reconnecting 3-4 times before the game just kills my hunter and sends me back to the start menu. With the perma-death* system in this game and the fact you lose equipment upon death makes this connection issue extremely frustrating. I'm fine with being out classed, lord knows I'm not that good at this game, but when I can't even get to the fights or my enemies are just lag-hopping around me, it makes it quite discouraging to play.  The menu screens glitch out a great deal as well, with changes either not registering or doing so multiple times to the point of game crash.

I'm quite tempted to remove this title from my library, which I hate because it genuinely can be quite fun and rewarding, but I haven't gotten to have that experience much in the 600 hours I've put into the game. Even at the discounted price I paid, I regret every penny I've spent on this game.

For a newcomer, this is one of the worst gaming experiences of all time, guys will just saw you from 150ft and headshot you, while you pixel hunt and wonder if you're aiming a guy or a leaf. Game could be fun but as of now for a newcomer stay away.
gunplay is not great, the aesthetic is nice until it starts getting in the way of the game. matchmaking takes too long. bosses are boring.
Keep coming back to the hunt. One of the most fun gameplay loops I've ever experienced.
One of the best shooter game!! I only wish there was more stuff to loot in the map, and quests to do.

it. is. fun.
layers of intertwined systems and a high learning curve to get good. pretty fun. definitely get it on sale, but if you love stealthy multiplayer games go ahead and go for it if not.
most amazing game i've ever played, pretty fun. 10/10 gunfights 9/10 weapons 10/10 mechanics. over all i would 100% recommend

Hunt Showdown changed my gaming expectations forever. I played PC games since Wolfenstein 3D and original Doom... This game triggers emotions I haven't felt since I was running around from Mr. X in resident evil 2 (original release on PC in 1999 timeframe) 

This game triggers the fight or flight response and every round is intense in its own way. The developers keep changing things that consistently modify the weapons gameplay, keeping you always on your toes and constantly trying new weapon loadouts. I've personally never played a game with so much interaction and changes that keep the game feeling "new".

I will never feel the original fear and excitement when I was totally clueless to the game, but I will say it brings out extreme frustration but also extreme excitement, depending if you win or lose... 

Do not buy this game if you just want an easy gameplay with minimal challenges.. every round keeps you on your toes and you need to understand that a 45 minute round could end in your death, but could have contained some of the most intense fighting showdowns you've ever encountered...

This game is not for the weak of heart... So many bad reviews based on liberal children who can't appreciate the mechanics that make the wins so epic and the losses so painful... 

Let me tell you, this game has some of the most beautiful landscapes... So immersive... I've stood by waterfalls and watched the God rays from the sun and got lost in the wilderness... Forgot I was looking for people... The sounds are so beautifully recorded... If you close your eyes, you would swear you are in nature... 

The mechanics? Unbelievable... This game uses sound as your tool... Listening to footsteps, gunshots, and the like... Key to every round. It takes people a while to adjust to it, but once you get it down, it's the best I've ever seen in any game. 

The game uses Autodesk (AutoCAD) to calculate bullet drop, bullet speed, and the like.... You need to calculate your shots and lead your enemy... Not like the games of old where you instantaneously hit your target from a mile away... The ballistics and behavior mimic real guns and ammo... I've dabbled in long distance shooting and this game taught me more on ballistics than many people at the gun ranges... It's amazing... It's also frustrating to players who are used to the older HALO, Fortnite cheesy instant long shots.... It takes practice... They've updated the shooting range in the game to help new players adjust and practice each gun...

This game triggers the fight or flight response in ways that I can never go back to games like Counter Strike or the like... I'm hooked...

Does this game have matchup issues? Sure... Some rounds you are thrown in with some of the most experienced people and you get smoked... Frustrating? Yes... But also a reminder that you're not that good and humbled by the experience

Other times you fight a bunch of similar skilled players and it's a long fought win or loss... I personally find it exciting whether I win or lose in those... My brothers? Sometimes they want to quit and never play again... But they always hit me up within a week of not playing to go back in to the Bayou... 

I love the slower weapons and the many skills that change your weapons behavior... Countless options to get good at! 

Did I mention the nature and how beautiful the environment is? Yes, because it simply is one is the best engines I've seen in a game.. the same company that brought you Crysis brings you Hunt and it's beautiful in ways no other game can compete with. 

Thanks Crytek! This game must continue to grow! I hope you do a new installment in the future with further upgrades to graphics in years to come... This game is amazing. Thank you for making every other game feel like child's play...

The game is really great; however, there is one major downside: why do I, at level 30, always have to compete against players with Prestige 100? Even the most clueless player, after spending enough hours in this game, can outgun you with a shotgun. I understand that they must be a pretty bad player if they’re still stuck at my rank, but it’s still frustrating.

Hunt is still great in 2026. Love the visceral and atmospheric style of the game. Frequent updates keep the game fresh and add new mechanics. From a gameplay perspective, I don't think it gets any better than this in the genre. Really, there are only small issues that hold the game back. My only major complaint is the UI, which must have been designed by Crytek's intern...

Fantastic game, one of the earlier extraction shooters and still an exceptionally unique experience.
If you have the time for the long matches and many mechanical peculiarities than it is absolutely worth it.
The setting of 19th century Louisiana is incredibly unique, an aesthetic for an incredibly interesting era of American history that I feel goes quite unrecognized.
Highly recommend.

The game is well designed and has always been fun, back when I was trying to get into it on console it took me a bit to warm up to it but that was on PS4 years ago when the game was even grungier and that 30 FPS felt awful. There's no other game that quite scratches the itch that Hunt does. The gun play alone is enough for me to always find my way back even if I take breaks from time to time. There's nothing like a cronchy headshot. Some times the devs take 1 step forwards and 2 steps back (laughs in revive bolts, Salveskin pre-patch) but that is just bound to happen with any live service game. At least the devs actually listen to their community by following their socials well, making surveys, and always trying to improve the game. I will say the cosmetics are becoming quite heavy and that bothers me here and there but I think they've done a mostly good job at making them grounded and authentic (some skins really don't belong like the one for Ghostface... like they could have at least made his mask carved out of wood or something). Overall I highly, highly recommend people play this game if they have even a modicum of appreciation for the wild west. Good luck out there Hunters and remember Hunt giveth and Hunt taketh.

Hard working devs, a very small team compared to these other games who dont add anything of substance or value. Do not listen to these whiners, nothing will ever be good enough for them. I hate most live service games but this is one of the few good ones ongoing. Game delivers every time on what it promises. Dev team very closely listens to community feedback from real players. Worth the price, def worth the sale price, and many free content options, even has generous pass/paid content.

31 grueling hours and yet I still haven't played the game 31 hours of clicking for 10 seconds getting shut down and trying again 31 TIMES everytime I boot it up it wont work tried everything this was my dream game if it was a dying wish it'd be to actually play this, I just want to play it :((
been playing this game for years on console and switched to pc recently makes all the difference... this game looks amazing for how old it is

Game goes f*cking hard.  The gunplay, the loadout/gadget variety, the audio design and immersion.. when it all comes together, there's absolutely nothing else like it. 

Every time I come back to it, it just never lets me down.

The Most Tense Shooter Ever Made

1. The tension is unmatched

Hunt doesn’t rely on cheap jump scares or scripted moments.
The fear comes from silence, from footsteps, from a branch snapping 40 meters away.

Every match feels like a slow, methodical infiltration.
You’re not a superhero.
You’re not a space wizard.
You’re a hunter with limited ammo, limited health, and unlimited anxiety.

This is the PvPvE tension that no other game replicates.

 2. The sound design is the best in the industry

Hunt’s audio is so precise you can:

    track footsteps through walls

    hear reloads across compounds

    identify guns by sound

    know exactly where a fight is happening

It’s the only game where listening is a skill as important as aiming.

This is the sound driven gameplay that makes Hunt legendary.

 3. The gunplay is grounded and unforgiving

No laser beams.
No bullet hoses.
No sci‑fi nonsense.

Just:

    black powder rifles

    lever actions

    break‑action shotguns

    slow reloads

    high lethality

Every shot matters.
Every miss matters more.

This is the 1890s arsenal that forces patience and precision.

4. The permadeath system gives every fight meaning

When your hunter dies, they’re gone.

Gear? Gone.
Traits? Gone.
Progress? Gone.

It sounds harsh, but it’s the reason Hunt feels alive.
You don’t run into fights brainlessly you think, you plan, you listen, you move with purpose.

This is the permadeath progression that makes every match unforgettable.

5. The world is atmospheric, oppressive, and unforgettable

Hunt’s Louisiana swamps feel like a character of their own:

    decayed farms

    rotting shacks

    fog covered fields

    burning compounds

    monsters lurking in the dark

It’s not just a map.
It’s a mood.

This is the setting that keeps you immersed.

6. The PvPvE design creates stories no other game can

You’re hunting a boss…
…but other players are hunting you.

You hear gunshots in the distance.
You hear dogs barking.
You hear crows take off.
You hear someone step on glass.

Suddenly the entire match becomes a psychological battle.

This is the emergent gameplay that keeps Hunt fresh years later.

 7. The teamwork feels real

Hunt isn’t about:

    K/D

    cosmetics

    power fantasy

It’s about:

    reviving your partner under fire

    whispering callouts

    holding your breath together

    pushing a compound as one

    surviving against impossible odds

You’re just two or three hunters trying to make it out alive.

 Final Verdict

Hunt: Showdown is the most unique FPS on the market.
It’s tense, grounded, atmospheric, and brutally fair.
It rewards patience, teamwork, and awareness  not flashy abilities or endless grind.

If you want a game where:

    the mission is clear

    the enemies are real players

    the stakes are high

    the tension is constant

    the gunfights are unforgettable

Then Hunt is the best shooter you can play today.

It’s not just a game.
It’s an experience.

I highly recommend the game if you're looking for an extraction shooter you can play with your friends. Playing solo is good too. The game can be brutal sometimes and seem unfair. Give the game a try. The community is very good too. I look forward to seeing you all in the Bayou!

Truly an incredible game. Took a long hiatus and came back after the 1896 update expecting everything to be different but was pleasantly surprised to find things improved. The sound design is spectacular and nothing will ever quite give that same rush of adrenaline as a heated fight in this game. It has a steep learning curve but well worth the effort of learning. Was a big fan, now I get an 0x30001 error on game bootup. Tried a lot of methods to fix but no ball. Can't be a positive review till I can play the game again.

An actually amazing game, I have much to say but I'll keep it short in a way.

Let's start with the negatives first, get the bad out of the way;
1st, Audio cues are EVERYTHING, so if you're new and playing music or a video, you better turn that down or off, as with distractions, you will miss footsteps, hunters gasps when they swing or jump, and hunters taking damage if they get hit by an infected.
2nd, Considering audio is everything, sometimes your audio will glitch, hasn't happened as MUCH thankfully as they do update this game pretty frequently it used to be bad and sometimes hunters wouldn't make a sound, but that seems to be in the past now thankfully, But I have encountered a few glitches/errors where infected won't make a sound (not even a detected sound) until they hit you.
3rd, This game has a VERY high skill roof, so if you're new, you might get outmatched fast, at least I was when I first played, but trust me, it gets better, after like 5-10 hours you start to get well adjusted to the game.
4th, The noise traps, THEY ARE EVERYWHERE, now you might think "well duh" but no, I mean if you're trying to get into a town or anything, there will be crows on one entrance, dogs on the next and maybe either a chicken coup or more crows on the last entrance, pretty much guaranteeing your chances of triggering them. Now maybe that's bad RNG for me! as I've seen that and unfortunately encountered it. But imo there's just so many sound traps too close together. But that doesn't mean I hate them, I like being forced to crouch between crows and dogs, makes the risk/reward WAYY more worth it.

Now let's get to the positives!
1st, Even though it's a high skill roof, It's not egregious, You will learn FAST, and will be able to keep up.
2nd, Earlier I did say if you were new, don't listen to music or videos, but honestly after like 10-15 hours of playing and getting used to the sound cues/audio, you can play music or videos, for me I just listen to some SmokeyMcC or Kiwi Tapes LOL.
3rd, I recommend setting your sight/model to center, yes I know the lowered state is the OG state of your weapon, I have been playing for a while now, I loved the lowered state, but honestly, I recommend the center state for any new players or just players who prefer that, I prefer it, even after (I believe) 1-2 years of playing this game.
4th, I LOVE how there is a skill tree/battle pass thing, where the more you level up, the more you unlock, IMO that makes leveling up so much more worth it, if anyone remembers, all the skills and perks used to be unlocked out the get go, this was fun, but honestly I prefer unlocking it as I progress, makes it more worth it and pushes the player to try harder instead of just running around with great perks and dying, but it's okay! cause he already has them unlocked at lvl 3 LOL.
5th, Honestly I just love the art style of this game, at well as the atmosphere.

Hunt: Showdown 1896 is proof some games are purely developed for adults. The aesthetics and gameplay create a mature, competitive experience that prioritizes skill over luck. A slower pace--most of your guns are single shot--leads to longer matches and memorable encounters that won't appeal to a younger audience, but scratch a particular itch for those who have it.
Best game of it's kind. Can't say one bad thing about it. There are accasional bugs, but nothing that would make the gameplay unpleasant. People are complaining about UI, I think it's decent.

The game is a good concept BUT when you disconnect from a match for some reason it's nearly impossible to reconnect. You have about 5% of success in reconnecting unless you wait for like 10 mins and by that time you're probably dead. This is frustrating as hell but a good game concept.
A real addicting game, only problem is the match making. Takes a while and you would get matched up against much higher stars. But over all, its a really good game.

They say this is about cowboys and zombies, but you can also be a plague doctor assassinating people with a crossbow, naked bushman charging at poor souls with a harpoon, or that one samurai who decides to come in dual-wielding two sets of revolvers loaded with explosive rounds. There's a lot a skill and playstyle expression besides aims and reactions, and it's all tied together by the heavy, grim visuals and sound design, which makes the spring from methodical exploration in the bayou to a blazing firefight all the more exhilarating.

The catch is it's a niche shooter that's inherently newbie-unfriendly. You gotta learn the clunky controls, know the guns and perks, understand all the spots in a compound, how to deal with every mob type, etc. etc. About the 300 hours mark, I found that mastering all these brought me immense dopamine unlike any other FPS out there.

If you have that kind of time or stubbornness to spare, I highly recommend Hunt. More importantly, make sure you swindle a few hardy friends to play with you because playing with randos is like shoving your hands into a loot crate from the bear trap factory. You can also join the official discord server. 70% of the demographics there (calculated by my biased eyes) are ex-military dads who love the 2nd amendment and hate their wife, which, by all means, is a great thing for some and terrible thing for others.

I bought this game a long time ago and refunded it after my first match. 1. I am exclusively solo 2. I am legally blind. I just didn't think I stood any chance against duos, trios and literally anyone who has better eyesight (which is almost everyone) I never stopped wanting to play. I love everything about the game- the aesthetic, the gameplay, the maps- everything. Since I refunded it, I've gotten a lot more experience under my belt with other FPS as well as MOBAs, PVP, etc. I was reading up on it and I saw people talking about the sound and how great it is and helpful for cues in the match. So... I bought it again. 

I was thinking- I'll go in full stealth build. I saw a lot of tips for new beginners- don't crouch around the map. Don't play stealth. I ignored that. I went into a match and extracted without going for the bounty- just to get a feel for things. I went in a second time, got the bounty and killed two other hunters Successful extraction, but I realized that my stealth build wasn't going to cut it. So, I went into the shooting range, changed my strategy and got killed pretty quickly in the very next match because I didn't quite have the right setup. Back to the drawing board. I now have two bounties under my belt where I killed all of the other hunters- duos. But, I maintained my stealth play style- so I could hear to see and get the upper hand and walk away the victor because those hunters were running around all willy nilly, making so much noise. I used that to my advantage and took them out. 

I'm fully aware that I won't always win, but this game is so much fun. So many playstyles. The realistic weapons and their reload animations. So many cool hunters. I'm so glad I gave this another chance. It was the right decision.
10/10 great hunts. even better showdowns. with guns and equipment from 1896 what more could you ask for.

This game's best days are obviously behind it. The matchmaking is horrible, constantly pitting the sweatiest 6 star 3-stack premades against average 3-4 star MMR players queuing separately. The gameplay loop has turned into a complete cringe-fest, strongly rewarding corner camping, and other low-skill, cheesy exploits, even in the fast-paced game mode. I'm glad I got hundreds of good hours in before this game went fully into the gutter, but Crytek is just trying to squeeze as many battlepass dollars out of it as they can while the lack of player base growth makes matches progressively lower quality and sweatier over time. Don't waste your time, and definitely don't waste your money. Don't get me wrong, this game has never been perfect, but in the past, there was real promise. All of the character and life this game used to have is pretty much gone now. This game is dying, if not already dead.

Not the direction we need.

I’m writing this review to help you save your money, time and nerves. This game is completely unplayable.


1)The game has a fundamentally flawed trigger/action system. In combat you can accidentally vault through a window when all you wanted to do was jump near it.

2)The collision system is broken. You can’t reliably jump onto objects and feel any solid physical surface. Some objects don’t even have collision at all, which leads to absurd situations where players literally clip and fall through textures.


3)The low tick rate and poor net-code make it feel like your shots and projectiles simply don’t register or reach the target properly.

4)There is little to no action taken against cheaters, and there’s no visible tracking for reports. It genuinely feels like reports are either never sent to the server or are completely ignored.


All four of these issues seriously ruin the experience and make the game incredibly frustrating to play. Until these problems are fixed, rage and disappointment are basically unavoidable. I strongly do not recommend buying this game.
